prerequisites: A Chift account with access to the Chift console (back-office) at chift.app.
An API Key created in the console, which provides the account_id, client_id, and client_secret values.
Choose the matching environment (sandbox or production), since API keys and the consumers they link to are environment-specific.
All three config fields are required: account_id, client_id, and client_secret.

authMethods.name: OAuth2 client credentials (token exchange)
authMethods.summary: Chift uses a token-based flow built on the account_id, client_id, and client_secret credentials. These three values are submitted to Chift's Create-a-token endpoint to obtain a JWT Bearer access token, which is then sent in the Authorization: BEARER {accessToken} header on subsequent API calls.

setupSteps.title: Create an API Key in the Chift console
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the Chift console and open the API Keys menu, then click Add API Key to generate a new key. Copy the account_id, client_id, and client_secret immediately, because the client_secret is shown only once during creation. You can optionally limit the key's scope to one or more consumers.

setupSteps.title: Collect the three credential values
setupSteps.body: From the API Keys menu, copy the account_id, the client_id, and the client_secret. These are the exact values the connector needs for its account_id, client_id, and client_secret fields. Use keys from the correct environment, since sandbox keys link to sandbox consumers and production keys link to production consumers.

setupSteps.title: Provide the credentials to the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter account_id, client_id, and client_secret into the connector configuration. The connector exchanges them at Chift's token endpoint for a JWT Bearer token and uses that token on each request.

sources.title: Chift Docs - Authenticate with Chift API (create API key)
